observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed, through the Municipal GAD FPS-TWG, to enhance the quality of its GPB and delivery of its GAD PPAs by:
a. adequately and appropriately establishing, maintaining, and keeping up-to-date its GAD Database through the implementation and utilization of the Community-Based Monitoring System and other practicable data gathering processes/tools; and
b. improving institutional knowledgebase through capacity building of GAD actors on nuances/matters/principles/technicalities such as, but not limited to, the RBM, project management, and gender-issue identification that affect GBP planning, implementation, monitoring, evaluation, and reporting.

We recommended and Management agreed to further enhance the delivery of results of its CoVID-19 initiatives through improving the quality of the related processes of planning and implementation by ensuring that essential dependencies, such as data availability and reliability, are adequately managed, and compliances to existing regulatory prescriptions are reasonably made.

We recommended and Management agreed, with the Offices of the Mayor, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer as leads, to reinforce the application of the rules and regulations pertaining to the grant, liquidation and/or settlement of cash advances to exact accountability by:
a. requiring approval on the face of the cash advance voucher that said official/employee has no preceding unliquidated cash advance;
b. issuing demand letter/s for advances already due and demandable and halt salary releases if the official/employee still fails to liquidate the cash advance within the specified time frame;
c. designating a Special Disbursing Officer (SDO) for advances for special undertaking for proper control and monitoring of the grants and liquidations; and
d. drawing necessary journal entries to appropriately recognize settlement of outstanding cash advances.

We recommended and Management agreed, with the Office of the Municipal Accountant as lead, to ensure the quality of related management processes as regards its cash advance, such as monitoring and decision-making, by having reliable and fairly presented information through properly classified accounts and appropriately maintained subsidiary ledgers.

We recommended and Management agreed, with the Offices of the Mayor and Municipal Accountant as leads, to ensure that cash advances of those who are and would-be separated from the service are settled by enforcing measures provided/allowed by exiting regulations, such as demands, clearances, and withholding of salaries.

observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2020 to submit their catch-up plan and affidavit of undertakings for the new commitment to complete the project. The LGU is currently keeping updating the CDP 20% DF is taken from AIP which is taken from LDIP and CDP to ensure allocated funds for specific project is sufficient to complete the project.
observations.reason: The contractor was required to submit their catch-up plan and affidavit of undertakings for the new commitment to complete the project.

observations.recommendation:
With the areas of improvement noted, the following advancement measures are co-developed with management:
a. Capacitate and enhance the General Services Office (GSO) by taking concrete actions to address issues related to manpower and formalization of the office to ensure effective and efficient management of all PPE owned by LGU;
b. The Municipal Accounting Office shall capacitate and analyze the current workloads of the manpower, and delegate the task on the establishment and maintenance and update of PPE Ledger Cards for reporting purposes; and the Supply Officer to set-up and maintain Property Cards for an efficient monitoring and reconciliations of the agency’s PPE with that of the accounting
c. Exhaust all efforts to locate relevant data pertaining to PPEs as a basis for recording in the book and providing proper depreciation to all depreciable assets. Moreover, adjustments should made accordingly; and
d. Institute mechanism controls over the safekeeping of all legal documents concerning LGU’s owned PPE. Trace the records and documents supporting the claim of ownership over parcels of land and ensure proper safekeeping thereof. Further, facilitate the preparation of all necessary documentary requirements to secure Transfer Certificate of Titles (TCTs) under the name of the Municipality in order to protect the land assets of possible risk of losing its assets.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY 2020 Waiting for the complete inventory of PPEs by the Inventory Committee. Details of the computation of Depreciation of can be found in Notes to Financial Statements.
Secured some the CTC of land owned by the LGU.
Please refer to Municipal Assessor reply to Ms. Merry Gen Cafino dated June 14, 2021 justification labelled as Annex xxxxx.

observations.recommendation:
With these observations noted in audit, we recommended the following:
a. Address the gaps/causes of all the deficiencies noted by the audit team in order to expedite the project implementation;
b. Discontinue charging of non-related expenses against the trust fund; and
c. The project management team created shall conduct close supervisions and monitoring in the progress of project implementation. Likewise, establish feedback mechanism and regulatory controls/measures to ensure completion of project within the given timeline.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2019
AAR CY 2018
AAR CY 2018

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant will look into the documents of the lacking P63,676.23 advances and make an analysis on such documents whether or not it will be included in the ageing schedule and make the necessary adjustments.

Therefore, we reiterated our recommendation to the following:
a. Exert effort to demand immediately the liquidation of all outstanding cash advances from the concern officials/employees who are still connected to the Municipality pursuant to COA Circulars 97-002 and 2012-004.
b. Impose sanction prescribed in the said Circular for non-compliance therefore, such as withholding the salaries and other emoluments of the officials/employees who continuously disregard, fail or decline to comply with the demand, including filing appropriate charges against them;
c. Send demand letters to officials and employees who are no longer connected with the Municipality but those whereabouts are still known to liquidate their cash advances and consider taking legal action/remedy against them.
d. Gather all documents that will prove that liquidation of the named individuals is no longer possible such are those who are already deceased (e.g. death certificate) and whose last address is not known (e.g. retirement certification, clearances, etc.) and request for the write off of the receivable for fair presentation of the account in the Financial Statements taking into consideration the liability of those individuals who granted the clearances.

Therefore, recommended the following:
a. Exert effort to secure the necessary documents of the payables that were not provided with details to identify which amount should be dropped from the books;
b. Confirm the amount of the payables from the list of the creditors identified to determine which of the payables are still valid and bonafide obligation of the Municipality.
c. Make necessary adjustments in the books of accounts in proven to be without existing creditors for an accurate presentation of the payable account in Financial Statements.
d. Trust Fund’s due to NGAs account identified as completed projects must be liquidated immediately.

Therefore, we recommended the following:
a. Exert effort to secure the necessary documents of the receivables account’s subsidiary ledger to support the balances of the account presented in the Financial Statements;
b. Confirm the reconstructed receivables to identify which of the receivables are still valid receivables of the Municipality.
c. Make the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts if proven to be without existing debtor for an accurate presentation of the other receivable account in the Financial Statements supported with complete documents.

Therefore, we recommended the following:
a. Enforce the provisions of the MOA, to wit:
• Ensure that projects identified by the barangay should be properly and carefully planned and studied by conducting a survey on the development issues pertaining the said barangay;
• Conduct the periodic visit to the projects site to ensure that actual implementation conforms to the approved plans and specifications.
• Freeze and recover the funds transferred from those recipient barangays who failed to adhere to the provisions stated in 2.3.
b. Include in the MOA a provision on the liquidation of the funds transferred in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-001.

Therefore, we recommended that the management enjoined the BAC to strictly comply the rules and regulations on the submission of contracts together with its supporting documents for timely conduct of contract review and technical inspection. We also request the management to submit the lacking documents related to the above project immediately to facilitate the completion of contract review.

Therefore, we recommended that the municipality strictly follow DILG-PCW- NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2013-01 specifically:
a. Follow the timeline for GAD Planning and Budgeting, Submission, Review and Endorsement of Agency GAD Plans and Budgets, Implementation and Monitoring of the GAD Plan and Budget, and Preparation and Submission of GAD Accomplishment Reports;
b. Conduct a study to collect data on the issues that concern women and maximize the GAD Budget to those programs that promote women empowerment, gender equality and gender and development; and
c. The GAD Focal Point should assume greater responsibility in record keeping, monitoring and evaluation of all PPAs included in the GAD Plan and Budget to properly consolidate and to cause prompt submission of reports.

Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials submit regularly the required reports/official receipts/disbursement vouchers and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor to facilitate timely verification and analysis of the accounts affected/appearing in the financial statements and to affirm the reliability of management’s assertions of existence, occurrence, validity, accuracy and completeness of the recorded transactions. Non-submission of these documents is sanctioned by Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.

We recommended that management strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 in future procurement undertakings and equip concerned officials especially the BAC with proper knowledge in carrying out functions, duties and responsibilities by attending series of seminars and trainings on RA 9184.

We recommended that management strictly adhere to the provision of Section 20, Volume I of the NGAS Manual in order to ensure reliability of the balances of Real Property Tax receivable and Special Education Tax receivable accounts. Setting up of RPT/SET receivable should be done based on the Certified List of Taxpayers.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs) in order to facilitate monitoring of tax collections and delinquencies and to segregate duties between access to recording and collection. Furthermore, record in the books the RPT/SET discounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Government issue policy guidelines in the hiring of job order personnel in accordance with Section 2, Rule XI of the Revised Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of EO No. 292 and grant the rates of salaries pursuant to Section 210, Volume I of GAAM, otherwise, said employees should be hired as casual or emergency staff. Further, if merited under existing Civil Service rules and regulations, the performance of the said job order personnel should be evaluated for possible appointment to permanent plantilla positions.

We recommended that the LGUs of the province further enhance the quality of its
individual LDRRM management processes by taking concrete actions to address the issues
related to manpower, data and information, and formalization to reinforce the
establishment of an enabling level of coordination in a collective setting and ensure disaster
resiliency.

a. Address the identified gap in the budgeting phase by conducting analysis on the
current workloads through unloading and/or fair distribution of the tasks of the
competent and technical people, who will prepare the POWs and plans and be
made available during budget deliberations;

b. Gaps/delays in the implementation phase be addressed by making constant
follow-ups with the input providers-suppliers for the immediate delivery of
construction materials. Otherwise, legal remedies are resorted to, if warranted;

c. Enhance the delivery of DF initiatives by making sure that the most prioritized
developmental PPAs whose results and outcomes address the most needed
interventions by the community, in general; and

d. Establish a feedback assessment mechanism to follow through the outcomes and
impacts of the delivered DF initiatives to the end-users vis-à-vis with the
identified baseline indicators/targets/objectives set beforehand.

a. Address the identified gap/causes in the non-compliances of the required supporting
documents to all government disbursements, otherwise there will be issuances of Notice of
Suspension, when warranted;

b. Gaps/delays in the implementation phase be addressed by making constant follow-ups with
the input providers-suppliers for the immediate and full delivery of approved PPAs.
Otherwise, legal remedies are resorted to, if warranted;

c. Enhance the delivery of LDRRM initiatives by making sure that the most prioritized
LDRRM PPAs whose results and outcomes address the most needed COVID-19
interventions by the community, in general; and

d. Establish a feedback assessment mechanism to follow through the outcomes and impacts of
the delivered LDRRM initiatives to the end-users vis-à-vis with the identified baseline
indicators/targets/objectives set beforehand.

With the areas of improvement noted, the following advancement measures are co-
developed with management:

a. Capacitate and enhance the General Services Office (GSO) by taking concrete
actions to address issues related to manpower and formalization of the office to
ensure effective and efficient management of all PPE owned by the LGU;

b. The Local Chief Executive shall capacitate and support its Inventory
Committee in conducting the complete physical count of all the agency’s
Property, Plant and Equipment taking into consideration the standard and
61

and guidelines provided in the COA Circular No. 2020-006 in order to reconcile the
PPE records and eventually arrive at the correct balances of the PPE Accounts;

c. The Municipal Accounting Office shall capacitate and analyze the current
workloads of the manpower, and delegate the task on the establishment and
maintenance and update of PPE Ledger Cards for reporting purposes; and the
Supply Officer to set-up and maintain Property Cards for an efficient
monitoring and reconciliations of the agency’s PPE with that of the accounting;

d. Exhaust all efforts to locate relevant data pertaining to PPEs as a basis for
recording in the book and providing proper depreciation to all depreciable
assets. Moreover, adjustments should made accordingly; and

e. Institute mechanism controls over the safekeeping of all legal documents
concerning LGU’s owned PPE. Trace the records and documents supporting
the claim of ownership over parcels of land and ensure proper safekeeping
thereof. Further, facilitate the preparation of all necessary documentary
requirements to secure Transfer Certificate of Titles (TCTs) under the name of
the Municipality in order to protect the land assets of possible risk of losing its
assets.

With these deficiencies, we recommended the following: a. Take up the following: Reclassifying/Adjusting journal Entries (PRAJE/PAJEs); b. Institute additional internal controls over the grant of cash advances for various employees’ salaries and payroll. Discontinue the grant of excessive advances for the said purpose by basing advance amount on net payroll only; c. Establish additional controls by opening and maintenance of a separate bank account for 20% Municipal Development Fund for Accountability and reporting purposes; d. Address all the causes of non-liquidations by all accountable employees and officers. Request for write-off of the accounts proven impossible of collectability; e. Establish additional monitoring mechanism by the LCE and Municipal Treasurer, with the latter to refrain from certifying cash availability in the Purchase Requests to support incurrence of payables at year-end in the absence of actual and available cash. The same should facilitate the transfer of funds from the GF to appropriate Trust Funds for accountability and monitoring; f. Create Inventory Committees to timely and strategically plan the conduct of complete physical count of PPE and prepare/submit the corresponding RCPPE and IRRUP and reconciles the results of the count with the GSO and accounting records; g. Exhaust all efforts to locate relevant data/information pertaining to the donated and disposed PPE as basis in the book recording; and h. Maintain the PPELCs and PCs to facilitate reconciliation and fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the books.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 1 AAR 2019 to take actions on the implementation of the recommendations provided in audit.
observations.reason: Concerned offices already started the implementation in audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGUs of the province collectively and individually ensure the effective and efficient delivery of LDRRM responsibilities by enhancing the level of coordination through managing the challenges on the dependencies that affect the essential management processes.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 2 AAR 2019 to take appropriate actions in the collective setting.
observations.reason: LGU to ensure an enhanced level of coordination in the collective setting.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGUs of the province ensure the effective and efficient execution of disaster responses through the establishment of appropriately contextualized disaster protocols.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 3 AAR 2019 to take appropriate actions.
observations.reason: The LGU has to take an action with respect to establishment of appropriately contextualized disaster protocols.

observations.recommendation: With these observations noted by the audit team, we recommended the following: a. Address the gaps/causes of all the deficiencies noted by the audit team to address the implementation; b. Discontinue charging of non-related expenses against the trust fund; and c. The project management team created shall conduct close supervisions and monitoring in the progress of project implementation. Likewise, establish feedback mechanism and regulatory controls/measures to ensure completion of project within the given timeline.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 4 AAR 2019 to take actions in addressing the gaps and expedite the implementation.
observations.reason: Though there was already a partnership with Barangay Council of Kuden, there is still increase in the accomplishment rate, there is still identified a problem with gaps and indigenous manpower that caused delays.

observations.recommendation: With these audit observations, we recommended the following: a. Immediately address the causes of all the systems deficiencies noted by the audit team in order to strengthen internal controls over cash receipts and disbursements, with the audit to generate reliable financial reports timely for management’s use in decision making; b. Discontinue the granting of cash advance for payment of employees’ salaries and wages equivalent to the gross amount or in excess of the net pays of the employees and cause the immediate refund of excess cash amounting to P2,500,743.02; c. Discontinue the use of payroll fund other than its intended purpose; d. Establish a well-balanced personnel workload plan in the Treasury Office concerned, where there is a fair distribution and elimination of unnecessary/redundancy of workloads, with consideration that highly sensitive work and those that entail accountability shall be assigned to regular/permanent employees only and restrict accounting personnel in the maintenance and recording of transactions in the cashbooks; e. Institute additional controls over cash receipts and disbursements by the Municipal Accounting and Treasury Office; and f. Strictly adhere to the existing rules and regulations specifically the Local Treasury Operations (LTO) Manual.
observations.managementAction: Finding No.1 AAR 2018 to take appropriate actions in full compliance with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasury Office (MTO) striving for full compliance.

observations.recommendation: With the foregoing observations, we recommended the following measures: a. In case of projects with contract, impose liquidated damages and terminate or rescind the contracts on infrastructure projects with negative slippage of more than 10 percent arising from contractor’s fault or negligence; b. Address the causes of the delays in the implementation of the projects in order to prevent further deprivation of the intended beneficiaries’ immediate and maximum use; c. Cause the immediate implementation of all the on-going and not yet started PPAs; and d. Strictly comply with the provisions of DBM-DILG JMC No. 2017-01 on the policies and guidelines on the utilization of the development fund.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 2 AAR 2018 to take appropriate measures.
observations.reason: Implemented projects subject to this observation are already for completion.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: a. Create an inspection and appraisal team to conduct a complete physical inventory of all assets owned by the Municipality; b. Maintain and update property tags and stock/property cards by the Property Custodian; c. Exert more efforts to secure the documents of properties not provided with depreciation; d. Identify unserviceable properties and record the same in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property to facilitate disposal and eventual dropping from the books; and e. Transfer all completed projects to General Fund and subject the same to depreciation.
observations.managementAction: Finding No.3 AAR 2018 to take appropriate actions.
observations.reason: Reiterated in AAR CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the following: 1. Create an inspection and appraisal team to conduct a complete physical inventory of all assets owned by the Municipality; 2. Maintain and update property tag and stock/property cards by the Property Custodian; 3. Exert more efforts to secure the documents of properties not provided with depreciation; 4. Identify unserviceable properties and record the same in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property to facilitate disposal and eventual dropping from the books; and 5. Transfer all completed projects to General Fund and subject the same to depreciation.
observations.managementAction: Finding No.1 of AAR 2017 to take appropriate actions in full compliance with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Reiterated in AAR CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation as last year’s: On comprehensive conduct of physical count: - Conduct a thorough physical count of PPE by the GSO and identify the properties counted under each category for easy reconciliation with the records of the Municipal Accountant; - Provide Property Numbers to each counted property with details; - Exert all efforts to secure the documents of properties not provided with cost and/or appraise all properties to provide with sound costing; - Issue/update ARE every three years after issuance to ensure property is still under the accountability of the accountable officer; - Issue Inventory Custodian Slip on properties considered as small tangible items; - Maintain and update PPELC and different property ledgers; - Maintain and update property cards by the Property Custodian; - Reconcile the PPELC, property cards and the physically counted properties; On properties not provided with depreciation - Exert all efforts to secure the documents of properties not provided with depreciation to have a knowledge of the date it was purchased to provide proper depreciation from date of purchase and/or appraise all properties not provided with depreciation with no retrieved documents and make a sound decision on the amounts of impairments; On Trust fund properties - Exert effort to locate documents related to the booked-up properties under Trust Fund; Secure documents related to PPEs recorded in the Trust Fund books; Transfer all completed projects’ properties to General Fund and subject the same to depreciation.
observations.managementAction: Finding To take appropriate actions in full compliance with the audit recommendations (AARs provided 2015, 2014).
observations.reason: Though the LGU already started initial efforts (e.g., issuance of AREs, initial inventory), difficulty in locating documents and full reconciliation persists.

observations.recommendation: In view of the observations/gaps noted, we reiterated our recommendation: Conduct an immediate and thorough physical count of PPE by designating personnel, follow the RPCPPE format as provided in Annex 49, Volume II, LGU-NGAS Manual; provide property numbers on physically counted assets; issue ARE on all properties counted; retrieve all necessary documents in the transfer of completed projects under SEF or Trust Fund and transfer the same to the General Fund and subject the transferred property to depreciation; maintain and update the PPELC as well as the property ledgers; maintain and update property cards by the Property Custodian; reconcile the PPELC, property cards and the physically counted properties; if budget permits, create a General Services Office (GSO) which will handle the Municipality’s properties; include Municipal properties not included in conversion from NGAS to PPSAS to present fairly the PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction: Finding Management to take appropriate actions (AAR 2015) and necessary actions to comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Reiterated in AAR CY 2020; management complied with recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality will conform to the NGAS process of flow of obligation based on Section 9 of the Manual to ensure that the amounts presented in the SAAOB are valid and correct. If the Municipal Treasurer wanted to confirm that budget is still available to a particular expense, an initial certification can be made by the Municipal Budget Officer but this does not equate as to certification indicated in the Obligation Request.
observations.managementAction: Finding No.7.2 of AAR 2015 to take appropriate actions in full compliance with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Validations and verifications from the management still ongoing; some obligations remain to be reconciled/validated.

Include in the MOA a provision on the liquidation of the funds transferred in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-001.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommend: • Validate and account for the details of the unidentified CIP amounting to P8,314,449.91 by coordinating closely with other concerned department heads particularly with the Municipal Engineer; • Make necessary adjustments to the books on identified completed projects with proper supporting documents; • Maintain separate CIPLC on per project basis; and • Ensure that only on-going and uncompleted projects are reflected in CIP.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 - Management to take appropriate actions: validate, reconcile and maintain per project subsidiary ledgers and CIPLC.
observations.reason: Reiterated in AAR CY 2020; management complied.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommend: • Coordinate with the Municipal Assessor and other concerned department heads to establish the list of total land holdings of the Municipality and their proper valuation; • Maintain the required RPLC for each class of real property; • Secure the original copies of the land holding of the Municipality from the Registry of Deeds and keep the same in a secured place.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 to take appropriate and necessary actions to comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Reiterated in AAR CY 2020; management complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 in future procurement undertakings and equip concerned officials especially the BAC with proper knowledge in carrying out functions, duties and responsibilities by attending series of seminars and trainings on RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 - Management to take appropriate actions and provide trainings on RA 9184 for the concerned officials.
observations.reason: Some compliance steps taken; still noted lack of complete documentation and issues resolved progressively.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Disposal Committee adhere and implement the COA Revised Guidelines on Appraisal of Property and prepare Agency Appraisal Report to facilitate proper evaluation and disposal of unserviceable properties and waste materials the soonest possible time. Also, that all unserviceable properties and waste materials be properly recorded and accounted for, and re-classified to Other Asset Account.

observations.reason: Reiterated in AAR CY 2020; management complied with disposal actions.

If merited under existing Civil Service rules and regulations, the performance of the said job order personnel should be evaluated for possible appointment to permanent plantilla positions.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 - Management to take appropriate actions and necessary steps to comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Management justified that due to personnel limitations it was necessary to hire job-order personnel; steps to regularize and comply continued.

observations.recommendation:

b. Institute additional internal control measures over the grant of cash advances
for employees’ salaries and payroll. Discontinue the grant of excessive advances for the
said purpose by basing cash advance amount on net payroll only;
c. Establish additional controls by opening and maintenance of a separate bank
account for 20% Municipal Development Fund for accountability and reporting purposes;
d. Address all the causes of non-liquidations by all accountable employees and
officers. Request for write-off of the accounts proven impossible of collectability;
e. Establish additional monitoring mechanisms by the LCE and Municipal
Treasurer, with the latter to refrain from certifying cash availability in the Purchase Requests to support
incurrence of payables at year-end in the absence of actual and available cash. The same
should facilitate the transfer of funds from the GF to appropriate Trust Funds for
accountability and monitoring;
f. Create Inventory Committees to timely and strategically plan the conduct of
complete physical count of PPE and prepare/submit the corresponding RCPPE and IRRUP and
reconciles the results of the count with the GSO and accounting records;
g. Exhaust all efforts to locate relevant data/information pertaining to the donated and
disposed PPE as basis in the book recording; and
h. Maintain the PPELCs and PCs to facilitate reconciliation and fair presentation of the PPE
accounts in the books.

We recommended that the LGUs of the province collectively and individually
ensure the effective and efficient delivery of LDRRM responsibilities by enhancing the level
of coordination through managing the challenges on the dependencies that affect the
essential management processes.

We recommended that the LGUs of the province ensure the effective and efficient
execution of disaster responses through the establishment of appropriately contextualized
disaster protocols.

With these observations noted in audit, we recommended the following:
a. Address the gaps/causes of all the deficiencies noted by the audit team to
expedite the project implementation;
b. Discontinue charging of non-related expenses against the trust fund; and
c. The project management team created shall conduct close supervisions and
monitoring in the progress of project implementation. Likewise, establish
feedback mechanisms and regulatory controls/ measures to ensure completion of
project within the given timeline.

With these audit observations, we recommended the following: a. Immediately address the causes of all the deficiencies noted by the audit team in order to strengthen internal controls over cash receipts and disbursements, and generate reliable financial reports timely for management’s use in decision making; b. Discontinue the grant of cash advance for payment of employees’ salaries and wages equivalent to the gross amount or in excess of the net pays of the employees and cause the immediate refund of excess cash amounting to P2,500,743.02; c. Discontinue the use of payroll fund other than its intended purpose; d. Establish a well-balanced personnel workload plan in the Treasury Office concerned, where there is a fair distribution and elimination of unnecessary/redundancy of workloads, with consideration that highly sensitive work and those that entail accountability shall be assigned to regular/permanent employees only and restrict accounting personnel in the maintenance and recording of transactions in the cashbooks; e. Institute additional controls over cash receipts and disbursements by the Municipal Accounting and Treasury Office; and f. Strictly adhere to the existing rules and regulations specifically the Local Treasury Operations (LTO) Manual.
observations.managementAction: F#1 AAR 2018 To take appropriate actions in full compliance with the audit recommendations
observations.reason: The deficiency still exists in the agency, hence striving for full compliance.

observations.recommendation: With the foregoing observations, we recommended the following measures: a. In case of projects implemented by contract, impose liquidated damages of P7,000,000.00 or 31.25% of the total appropriation under the 20% Municipal Development Fund or terminate or rescind the contracts on infrastructure projects with negative slippage of more than 10 percent arising from contractor’s fault or negligence; b. Address the causes of the delays in the implementation of the projects in order to prevent further deprivation of the intended beneficiaries’ immediate and maximum use; c. Cause the immediate implementation of all the ongoing and not yet started PPAs; and d. Strictly comply with the provisions of DBM-DILG JMC No. 2017-01 on the policies and guidelines on the utilization of the development fund.
observations.managementAction: F#2 AAR 2018 To take appropriate actions in compliance with the audit recommendations
observations.reason: Unimplemented projects subject to this observation are already for completion.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: a. Create an inspection and appraisal team to conduct a complete physical inventory of all assets owned by the Municipality; b. Maintain and update property tags and stock/property cards by the Property Custodian; c. Exert more efforts to secure the documents of properties not provided with depreciation; d. Identify unserviceable properties and record the same in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property to facilitate the disposal and eventual dropping from the books; and e. Transfer all completed projects to General Fund and subject the same to depreciation.
observations.managementAction: F#3 AAR 2018 To take appropriate actions in full compliance with the audit recommendations
observations.reason: Management did not comply all of the recommendations provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the following: 1. Create an inspection and appraisal team to conduct a complete physical inventory of all assets owned by the Municipality; 2. Maintain and update property tag and stock/property cards by the Property Custodian; 3. Exert more efforts to secure the documents of properties not provided with depreciation; 4. Identify unserviceable properties and record the same in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property to facilitate the disposal and eventual dropping from the books; and 5. Transfer all completed projects to General Fund and subject the same to depreciation.
observations.managementAction: F#1 AAR 2017 To take appropriate actions in full compliance with the audit recommendations
observations.reason: Management did not comply all of the recommendations provided. the maintenance and updating of stock/property cards and identification of unserviceable properties because no permanent GSO personnel assigned to focus and concentrate on PPE management.

observations.recommendation: Therefore. In view of the above observations/gaps of the Municipality, we recommended the following: • On comprehensive conduct of physical count: - Conduct a thorough physical count of PPE headed by the GSO and identify the properties counted under each category for easy identification of properties for reconciliation with the Municipal Accountant - Follow the RPCPPE format as provided in Annex 49, Volume II, LGU-NGAS Manual; - Provide Property Numbers to each counted property with details; - Exert all efforts to secure the documents of properties not provided with depreciation and make a sound decision on the amounts of impairments to present fairly the balances of the PPE and then provide the depreciation. - Issue/update ARE every three years after issuance to ensure that property is still under the accountability of the accountable officer to whom property was issued; - Issue Inventory Custodian Slip on properties considered as small tangible items pursuant to COA Circular 2005-02 (Annex B) in the coordination with COA related expenses Circular No. 97-005 (Annex C); - Maintain and update the PPELC as well as the different property ledgers (soft copy is acceptable which could be readily produced upon request for reports needed); - Maintain and update property cards by the Property Custodian (soft copy is acceptable); - Reconcile the PPELC, property cards and physically counted properties; - If budget permits, create a General Services Office (GSO) which will undertake the coordination with the management; - Exert all efforts to locate documents related to the booked-up properties under Trust Fund; - Secure documents related to PPEs recorded in the Trust Fund books, if none can be retrieved from the file of the Municipality, by communicating with the funding agency; and - Transfer all completed projects’ properties to General Fund and subject the same to depreciation.
observations.managementAction: F#1.1 AARs 2016 2015 2014 To take appropriate actions in full compliance with the audit recommendations
observations.reason: Unimplemented due to no permanent GSO personnel assigned to focus and concentrate on PPE management. Difficulty in locating the desired documents.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant will look into the documents of the advances lacking and make an analysis on such documents whether or not it will be included in the ageing schedule and make the necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction: F#2 of AAR 2016 To take appropriate actions in compliance with the audit recommendations
observations.reason: Not Difficulty in locating the desired documents.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended the following: a. The management should reconsider its loan agreements for their employees taking into account the provision of the GAA for the minimum net take home pay. b. The management should follow the order of priority in deducting mandatory and allowable deductions to the salaries of employees.
observations.managementAction: F#7 of AAR 2016 To take appropriate actions in compliance with the audit recommendations

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we reiterated our recommendation to the following: 1. Exert effort to demand immediately the liquidation of all outstanding cash advances from the concern officials/employees who are still connected to the Municipality pursuant to COA Circulars 97-002 and 2012-004. 2. Impose sanction prescribed in the said Circular for non-compliance therefore, such as withholding the salaries and other emoluments of the officials/employees who continuously disregard, fail or decline to comply with the demand, including filing appropriate charges against them. 3. Send demand letters to officials and employees who are no longer connected with the Municipality but those whereabouts are still known to liquidate their cash advances and consider taking legal action/remedy against them. 4. Gather all documents that will prove that liquidation of the named individuals is no longer possible such are those who are already deceased (e.g. death certificate) and whose last address is not known (e.g. retirement certification, clearances, etc.) and request for the write off of the receivable for fair presentation of the account in the Financial Statements taking into consideration the liability of those individuals who granted the clearances.
observations.managementAction: F#1.2 AAR 2015 To take appropriate actions in compliance with the audit recommendations
observations.reason: This deficiency still exists.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, recommended the following: a. Exert effort to secure the necessary documents of the payables that were not remain to be lodged in the payable accounts for the long period of time; b. Confirm the amount of the payables from the list of creditors identified to determine which of the payables are still valid and bonafide obligation of the Municipality. c. Make necessary adjustments in the books of accounts in proven to be without existing creditors for an accurate presentation of the payable account in Financial Statements. d. Trust Fund’s due to NGAs account identified as completed projects must be liquidated immediately.
observations.managementAction: F#4 of AAR 2015 To take appropriate actions in compliance with the audit recommendations
observations.reason: Difficulty in locating the desired documents.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, recommended that the following: a. Exert effort to secure the necessary documents of the receivables account’s subsidiary ledger to support the balances of the account presented in the Financial Statements; b. Confirm the reconstructed receivables to identify which of the receivables are still valid receivables of the Municipality. c. Make the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts if proven to be without existing debtor for an accurate presentation of the other receivable account in the Financial Statements supported with complete documents.
observations.managementAction: F# of AAR 2015 To take appropriate actions in compliance with the audit recommendations
observations.reason: Difficulty in locating the desired documents.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended that the Management change the scheme of the remittance of the collection of collectors to the Liquidating Officer in accordance to Section 29 of NDAS Volume 1, that is, to remit to the Liquidating Officer.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015

observations.recommendation: In view of the observations/gaps noted, we reiterated our recommendation on the same findings as last year’s: • Conduct an immediate and thorough physical count of PPE by designating personnel, who is not handling cash transactions, to do the activity. Follow the RPCPPE format as provided in Annex 49, Volume II, LGU-NGAS Manual; • Provide property numbers on physically counted properties for identity of the properties; • Issue ARE on all properties counted, which were in the hands of accountable officers; • Retrieve all necessary documents in the transfer of completed projects under SEF or Trust Fund and transfer the same to the General Fund books. Subject the transferred property to depreciation; • Maintain and update the PPELC as well as the different property ledgers; • Maintain and update property cards by the Property Custodian; • Reconcile the PPELC, property cards and physically counted properties; • If budget permits, create a General Services Office (GSO) which will handle the Municipality’s properties. Further, we recommended that the Municipal properties identified above that were not included in the conversion from NGAS to PPSAS be included to present fairly the PPE accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 2014 To take appropriate actions in full compliance with the audit recommendations
observations.reason: Management did not comply all of the recommendations provided particularly on the maintenance and updating of stock/property cards and property ledgers and the documentary requirements and validation. The management incurred delays in the submission of required reports.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended that the Municipality will conform to the NGAS process of flow of obligation based on Section 9 of the Manual to ensure that that amounts presented in the SAAOB are valid and correct. If the Municipal Treasurer really wanted to confirm that budget is still available to a particular obligating the expense, an initial certification based on the Purchase Request can be made by the Municipal Budget Officer but this does not equate as to certification indicated in the Obligation Request.
observations.managementAction: F# of AAR 2015 To take appropriate actions in compliance with the audit recommendations
observations.reason: Still locating and reconciling necessary supporting documents and validations from the management.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended the following: a. Enforce the provisions of the MOA, to wit: • Ensure that projects identified by the barangay should be properly and carefully planned and studied by conducting a survey on the development issues pertaining the said barangay; • Conduct the periodic visit to the projects site to ensure that actual implementation conforms to the approved plans and specifications. • Freeze and recover the funds transferred from those recipient barangays who failed to adhere to the provisions stated in 2.3. b. Include in the MOA a provision on the liquidation of the funds transferred in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: F#8 of AAR 2015 To take appropriate actions in compliance with the audit recommendations
observations.reason: Validations and verifications from the management and to the recipient barangays already started.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended that the management enjoined the BAC to strictly comply the rules and regulations on the submission of contracts together with its supporting documents for timely conduct of contract review and inspection. We also request the management to submit the lacking documents immediately to facilitate the completion of contract review.
observations.managementAction: F#10 of AAR 2015 To take appropriate actions in compliance with the audit recommendations
observations.reason: On process of complying the lacking documents noted.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended the following: a. The management should closely monitor the utilization and implementation of priority projects for the attainment of desirable socio-economic and environmental management outcomes and to maximize the usage of the fund. b. The implementing department concerned should fast track the implementation based on the scheduled time frames indicated in the AIP of the management to achieve the desired outcomes. c. Strictly comply with Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the Department of Interior and Local Government and Department of Budget and Management dated April 13, 2011.
observations.managementAction: F#12 of AAR 2015 To take appropriate actions in compliance with the audit recommendations
observations.reason: Unimplemented projects subject to this observation are already for completion.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended that the municipality strictly follow DILG-PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2013-01 specifically: a. Follow the timeline for GAD Planning and Budgeting, Submission, Review and Endorsement of Agency GAD Plans and Budgets, Implementation and Monitoring of the GAD Plan and Budget, and Preparation and Submission of GAD Accomplishment Reports; b. Conduct a study to collect data on the issues that concern women and maximize the GAD Budget to those programs that promote women empowerment, gender equality and development; c. The GAD Focal Point should assume greater responsibility in record keeping, monitoring and evaluation of all PPAs included in the GAD Plan and Budget to properly consolidate and to cause prompt submission of reports.
observations.managementAction: F#13 of AAR 2015 To take appropriate actions in compliance with the audit recommendations
observations.reason: Management complied with the documentary requirements and will undergo more trainings on GAD for effective implementation.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended that as a withholding agent of the Government, the management must compute, deduct and remit the exact amount of withholding tax pursuant to the January 1, 2009 table to conform with the Tax Reform Act of 1997 and to ensure that employees will not be burdened by paying high amounts of taxes that need to be paid in lump sum at year end and possible penalties for late payment of taxes on compensation.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials submit regularly the required reports/official receipts/disbursement vouchers and their supporting documents to the Office of the Disbursement Auditor to facilitate timely verification and analysis of the accounts affected/appearing in the financial statements; Non-submission of these documents is sanctioned by Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014
observations.reason: Management already incurred delays in the submission of required reports.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant will do the following: • Validate and account for the details of the unidentified CIP amounting to P8,314,449.91 by coordinating closely with other concerned department heads particularly with the Municipal Engineer; • Make necessary adjustments to the books on identified completed projects with proper supporting documents; • Maintain separate CIPLC on per project basis; and • Ensure that only ongoing and uncompleted projects are reflected in CIP.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014
observations.reason: Management to take actions to comply with the audit recommendations and to locate and validate supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommend the following to ensure fair presentation of the land account in the financial statement and will not be exposed to possible third party claims: • Coordinate with the Municipal Assessor and other concerned department heads to establish the list of total land holdings of the Municipality and their proper valuation; • Maintain the required RPLC for each class of real property; and • Secure the original copies of the land holding of the Municipality from the Registry of Deeds and keep the same in a secured place.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014
observations.reason: Management is having difficulty in securing land titles since most were donations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 in future procurement undertakings and equip concerned officials especially the BAC with proper functions, duties and responsibilities by attending series of seminars and trainings on RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 2014 To take appropriate actions in compliance with the audit recommendations
observations.reason: Management still having process issues and some lacking documents. Payments and procurement activities had deficiencies including non-forfeiture of bid security, late submission of performance bond, un-deducted liquidated damages, and payment prior to acceptance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Disposal Committee adhere and implement the COA Revised Guidelines on Appraisal of Property and prepare Agency Appraisal Report to facilitate proper evaluation and disposal of these unserviceable properties and waste materials the soonest possible time. Also recommended that all unserviceable properties and waste materials be properly recorded and accounted for, and re-classified to Other Asset Account.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 To take appropriate actions in compliance with the audit recommendations
observations.reason: Management did not comply all of the recommendations provided; no permanent GSO personnel assigned to focus on PPE management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management strictly adhere to the provision of Section 20, Volume I of the NGAS Manual in order to ensure reliability of the balances of the Real Property Tax receivable and Special Education Tax receivable accounts. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs) in order to facilitate monitoring of tax collections and delinquencies and to segregate duties between access to recording and collection. Furthermore, record in the books the RPT/SET discounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 To take appropriate actions in compliance with the audit recommendations
observations.reason: Management has not fully submitted the certified list; ongoing actions to set up subsidiary ledgers and segregation of duties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Government issue policy guidelines in the hiring of job order personnel in accordance with Section 2, Rule XI of the Revised Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of EO No. 292 and Section 210, Volume I of GAAM, and grant the rates of salaries pursuant to Section 210. Otherwise, said employees should be hired as casual or emergency staff. Further, if merited under existing Civil Service rules and regulations, the performance of the said job order personnel should be evaluated for possible appointment to permanent plantilla positions.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 To take appropriate actions in compliance with the audit recommendations
observations.reason: This deficiency still exists in the agency.
